title: Environment and Society
description: Talk about the big abstract topics at B1: the environment (climate change, pollution, recycling, renewable energy, sustainability) and society (equality, poverty, immigration, volunteering, rights, solidarity). Learn to frame problems and propose solutions with hay que, deberíamos, es necesario que + subjunctive and si recicláramos... Includes the classic false friend sensible (= sensitive, not sensible).

cards.notes: *El medio ambiente* is the natural world around us. Note it is **two words** and masculine: *proteger el medio ambiente* (to protect the environment). *Cuidar el medio ambiente* = to look after the environment.

cards.front: el cambio climático / el calentamiento global
cards.notes: *El cambio climático* (climate change) and *el calentamiento global* (global warming) are the two most common terms. *Climático* is an adjective here; the noun is *el clima* (the climate, masculine despite the -a).

*La contaminación* = pollution (air, water, etc.). The verb is *contaminar* (to pollute). Careful: *contaminación* is **pollution**, not 'contamination' in the food/medical sense, though context can overlap.
*El reciclaje* (recycling), verb *reciclar* (to recycle). *Los residuos* = waste, refuse; *la basura* is everyday rubbish/trash. *Reducir los residuos* = to reduce waste.
*Las energías renovables* (renewable energy, often plural in Spanish): *solar, eólica* (wind). *La sostenibilidad* = sustainability; the adjective *sostenible* = sustainable.
*La igualdad* (equality), opposite *la desigualdad* (inequality). *La pobreza* (poverty), adjective *pobre*. *Luchar contra la pobreza* = to fight against poverty.
*La inmigración* (immigration) — note **inm-**, not 'imm-' as in English. *Los derechos* = rights: *los derechos humanos* (human rights). *Un derecho* is also a noun meaning a right/entitlement.
*El voluntariado* = volunteering / voluntary work; *un voluntario / una voluntaria* = a volunteer. *La solidaridad* = solidarity; *ser solidario* = to be supportive/caring of others.

**False friend!** Spanish *sensible* means **sensitive** (emotionally or physically), e.g. *Es muy sensible a las críticas* (He's very sensitive to criticism). To say English 'sensible' (reasonable, level-headed), use **sensato** or **razonable**: *una decisión sensata* (a sensible decision). Don't say *una decisión sensible* for a wise choice.

cards.back: one must recycle / we should recycle
cards.notes: **Hay que + infinitive** = impersonal 'one must / it is necessary to' (no specific subject): *Hay que reciclar más*. Contrast with **tener que + infinitive**, which is personal: *Tengo que reciclar* (I have to). **Deberíamos + infinitive** (conditional of *deber*) = a softer 'we should / we ought to'.

cards.back: It is necessary that we all recycle. (subjunctive!)
cards.notes: Impersonal value judgements — *es necesario que, es importante que, es fundamental que* — trigger the **subjunctive**: *Es necesario que todos **reciclemos***. English speakers often wrongly use the indicative (*reciclamos*). If there is no *que* and no named subject, use the infinitive instead: *Es necesario reciclar*.

cards.front: Si todos recicláramos, habría menos residuos.
cards.back: If we all recycled, there would be less waste. (hypothetical)
cards.notes: For a hypothetical / unlikely situation, use **si + imperfect subjunctive, ... + conditional**: *Si todos **recicláramos**, **habría** menos residuos*. Connectors of cause/consequence: **debido a** (due to) + noun, and **por lo tanto** (therefore): *Hay mucha contaminación; **por lo tanto**, debemos actuar*.

steps.title: Talking about the planet and society
steps.body:
# Environment and society

At B1 you start handling **abstract topics**: the environment and the big issues facing society. This lesson gives you the vocabulary and the grammar to discuss problems calmly and to propose solutions.

## The environment — el medio ambiente

| Spanish | English |
|---|---|
| el medio ambiente | the environment |
| el cambio climático | climate change |
| el calentamiento global | global warming |
| la contaminación | pollution |
| el reciclaje · reciclar | recycling · to recycle |
| los residuos | waste |
| las energías renovables | renewable energy |
| la sostenibilidad · sostenible | sustainability · sustainable |
| proteger / cuidar el planeta | to protect / look after the planet |

## Society — la sociedad

| Spanish | English |
|---|---|
| la igualdad / la desigualdad | equality / inequality |
| la pobreza | poverty |
| la inmigración | immigration |
| el voluntariado | volunteering |
| los derechos (humanos) | (human) rights |
| la solidaridad | solidarity |

> Tone matters: present a problem (*un problema grave* = a serious problem) **and** a constructive solution. Avoid alarmism.

steps.title: Proposing solutions: hay que, deberíamos, es necesario que
steps.body:
## Three ways to recommend action

**1. hay que + infinitive** — impersonal 'one must / it is necessary to'. No specific person is named:

- *Hay que reducir los residuos.* (One must reduce waste.)

> Don't confuse it with **tener que + infinitive**, which is *personal*: *Tengo que reciclar* (I have to recycle). Use *hay que* for general truths, *tener que* for a named subject.

**2. deberíamos + infinitive** — the conditional of *deber*, a soft 'we should / we ought to':

- *Deberíamos usar más energías renovables.*

**3. es necesario que + subjunctive** — value judgements take the **subjunctive**:

- *Es necesario que todos **reciclemos**.* (not *reciclamos*)
- *Es importante que la gente **participe**.*

The same goes for *es importante que, es fundamental que, es bueno que*. English speakers often slip into the indicative here — watch the mood.

> If there is **no** *que* and no named subject, drop back to the infinitive: *Es necesario **reciclar***.

steps.title: Hypotheticals and connectors
steps.body:
## 'If we all recycled...' — the hypothetical

To imagine an unlikely or counterfactual situation, use:

**si + imperfect subjunctive, ... + conditional**

- *Si todos **recicláramos**, **habría** menos residuos.* (If we all recycled, there would be less waste.)
- *Si **usáramos** energías renovables, **contaminaríamos** menos.*

The imperfect subjunctive of *-ar* verbs: *reciclara, reciclaras, reciclara, recicláramos, reciclarais, reciclaran*.

## Cause and consequence connectors

| Connector | Meaning | Use |
|---|---|---|
| **debido a** | due to / because of | + noun: *debido a la contaminación* |
| **por lo tanto** | therefore / so | links a consequence |

- *Hay mucha contaminación **debido al** tráfico.*
- *Los residuos aumentan; **por lo tanto**, debemos actuar.*

## A false friend to watch

Spanish **sensible** means **sensitive**, not 'sensible'! *Es muy sensible* = He is very sensitive. For English 'sensible' (reasonable, level-headed), use **sensato / razonable**: *una solución sensata* = a sensible solution.

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish term with its English meaning.
Translate: *It is a sensible decision.* (sensible = reasonable, so use sensata!)
Fill in the present subjunctive of *reciclar* for nosotros.
exercise.sentence: Es necesario que todos ___ más para reducir los residuos.
exercise.hint: es necesario que triggers the subjunctive; reciclar → reciclemos (nosotros), not reciclamos.

steps.title: Build: one must protect the environment

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *One must protect the environment.* (impersonal hay que)
exercise.hint: hay que + infinitive = impersonal 'one must'; medio ambiente = the environment.

exercise.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'We should use more renewable energy'?

images.label: Debemos que usar más energías renovables.

distractors: Debemos que usar más energías renovables.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *If we all recycled, there would be less waste.* (si + imperfect subjunctive, conditional)
accept: Si todos recicláramos, habría menos residuos.
si todos recicláramos, habría menos residuos
Si todos reciclaramos, habria menos residuos.
Si todos recicláramos habría menos residuos.
Si todos reciclásemos, habría menos residuos.
distractors: Si todos reciclamos, hay menos residuos.
Si todos reciclaríamos, habría menos residuos.
Si todos recicláramos, hubiera menos residuos.

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish term with its English meaning.
Fill in the cause connector meaning 'due to'.
exercise.sentence: Hay más contaminación ___ al aumento del tráfico.
exercise.hint: 'due to' + noun = debido a (here debido a + el = debido al).

exercise.prompt: Translate: *One must fight against poverty.* (impersonal, no named subject → hay que)
accept: Hay que luchar contra la pobreza.
hay que luchar contra la pobreza
Hay que luchar contra la pobreza
exercise.hint: Impersonal 'one must' = hay que + infinitive (not tener que, which is personal).
distractors: Tiene que luchar contra la pobreza.
Hay que luchamos contra la pobreza.
Tenemos que luchar contra la pobreza.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *It's important that society participate.* (es importante que + subjunctive)
accept: Es importante que la sociedad participe.
es importante que la sociedad participe
Es importante que la sociedad participe.
exercise.hint: es importante que triggers the subjunctive; participar → participe (la sociedad).
distractors: Es importante que la sociedad participa.
Es importante que la sociedad sea sensible.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the connector meaning 'therefore / so'.
exercise.sentence: Los residuos aumentan cada año; por lo ___, debemos actuar ya.
